incorporations.original: my full name was hurled squarely at my chest
incorporations.replacement: I felt my full name hurled squarely at my chest
incorporations.reason: The Italian 'mi sentii lanciare' is reflexive — 'I felt [it] hurled at me' — not a passive 'was hurled'. Gemini's 'I felt' restores the first-person sensation of the original.
The Italian 'salutato militarmente' means he received a salute; 'military honours' inflates the sense beyond what 'militarmente' conveys.

incorporations.original: his great belly wrapped in a white apron
incorporations.replacement: his great paunch wrapped in a white apron
incorporations.reason: 'Epa' specifically means paunch (a protruding gut), not just belly. Gemini's 'paunch' is the more precise anatomical register.

incorporations.original: Several customs officers attended her table
incorporations.replacement: Several finance guards attended her table
incorporations.reason: 'Guardie di finanza' are finance guards (fiscal police), not customs officers. They are a distinct corps; Gemini's literal rendering is historically accurate.

incorporations.original: the façade of the magnificent church gleaming white before me
incorporations.replacement: the façade of the magnificent temple gleaming white before me
incorporations.reason: The Italian is 'magnifico tempio'. Crespi deliberately chose the classicising 'tempio' over 'chiesa'; 'temple' preserves that rhetorical elevation.

incorporations.original: I was once again in order
incorporations.replacement: I was once again in fine fettle
incorporations.reason: 'Di bel nuovo in assetto' has a colloquial jauntiness that 'in order' misses. Gemini's 'in fine fettle' captures the idiomatic tone better.

incorporations.original: he was a decorator, like the poor Antonio Sciesa
incorporations.replacement: he was an upholsterer, like the poor Antonio Sciesa
incorporations.reason: Neither draft was used. Claude had 'decorator', Gemini had 'tapestry-maker'. The Italian 'tappezziere' most accurately means upholsterer — and Antonio Sciesa is historically documented as an upholsterer. Both drafts were imprecise.

edgren_influences.note: Edgren's 'custom-place' confirmed that 'dazio' denotes the physical checkpoint, not just the duty owed. This supported 'customs-post' (a period-appropriate compound) over the Gemini draft's 'custom-house' or a modern 'toll-booth'.

A modern default for 'mite' might be 'mild' or 'meek'. Edgren's gloss includes 'gentle', which fits the collocation 'gentle figure' more naturally than 'mild figure' and avoids the connotation of passivity in 'meek'.

The Italian 'schioppettata' (from 'schioppo', musket) needed a period-appropriate English equivalent. Edgren's entry for the related 'colpo di fucile' = 'gunshot' confirmed this as the natural 1900-era English, avoiding a modern 'rifle shot' or 'bullet'.
